name: Robert Farnon
description: Canadian-born composer, conductor, musical arranger and trumpet player
abstract: Robert Joseph Farnon (24 July 1917 – 23 April 2005) was a Canadian-born composer, conductor, musical arranger and trumpet player. As well as being a composer of original works (often in the light music genre), he was commissioned by film and television producers for theme and incidental music.

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I said my technique consists mostly of stealing everything that I heard of Robert Farnon’s that I could capture.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded for the greatest vocal writer that ever lived, Robert Farnon. I did his “Porgy & Bess.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Herbie and I used to listen to all those kind of things, and me being dumb I made a mistake and said, “Oh by the way, Herbie, I stealed your two Robert Farnon records.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: listen to anything Johnny Mandel wrote or Robert Farnon who was the daddy of all the arrangers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]
